The following is a product launch press release. Read it carefully and answer the question at the end.

---
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

NovaSphere Technologies Unveils the CloudPad Pro: The Future of Creative Computing

SAN FRANCISCO, CA – NovaSphere Technologies today announced its most ambitious product to date: the CloudPad Pro, a professional-grade tablet designed for creative professionals, architects, and enterprise teams worldwide.

The CloudPad Pro features a 13-inch OLED display with a peak brightness of 1600 nits, a 120Hz ProMotion refresh rate, and full DCI-P3 color support. At its core is NovaSphere's proprietary M4 chip, delivering up to 40% faster neural processing compared to its predecessor, enabling real-time 4K video editing, complex 3D rendering, and AI-powered creative tools. The device supports the new NovaSphere Pencil 4, which offers sub-millisecond latency and 4096 levels of pressure sensitivity.

"The CloudPad Pro represents years of collaboration with professional users across industries," said CEO Amara Chen. "We built this device from the ground up based on feedback from thousands of creators who needed desktop-class power in a portable form factor."

Pricing starts at $1,099 for the base model (16GB RAM, 256GB storage) and scales to $1,899 for the top configuration (32GB RAM, 2TB storage). A cellular variant is available for an additional $150.

The CloudPad Pro will be available for purchase starting June 3, 2026, through the NovaSphere online store and authorized retail partners in the US, Canada, UK, Germany, France, Japan, South Korea, and Australia. Pre-orders open on May 20, 2026. NovaSphere will host a live launch event on May 15, 2026, streamed at novasphere.com/live.

About NovaSphere Technologies: Founded in 2014 and headquartered in San Francisco, NovaSphere is known for its CloudPad, CloudPhone, and NovaWatch product lines, with over 120 million active users worldwide.
---

Question: What is the launch date of the product? Answer with only the date.

The following is a quarterly financial report summary. Read it carefully and answer the question at the end.

---
MERIDIAN GLOBAL HOLDINGS — Q1 2026 FINANCIAL RESULTS

Meridian Global Holdings reported robust financial performance for Q1 2026, driven by strong demand across its cloud infrastructure and enterprise software divisions.

Total Revenue: Meridian reported total revenue of $4.82 billion for Q1 2026, compared to $3.91 billion in Q1 2025. This represents year-over-year revenue growth of 23.3%, exceeding analyst consensus estimates of $4.6 billion and marking the fifth consecutive quarter of accelerating top-line growth.

Segment Breakdown: The Cloud Infrastructure segment generated $2.14 billion in revenue (up 31% YoY), driven by increased enterprise adoption of Meridian's sovereign cloud solutions. The Enterprise Software segment contributed $1.67 billion (up 18% YoY). Professional Services recorded $1.01 billion (up 12% YoY).

Profitability: Operating income reached $891 million (18.5% margin, up from 15.2% prior year). Net income was $743 million, or $1.84 per diluted share. Adjusted EBITDA was $1.23 billion (25.5% margin).

Balance Sheet: As of March 31, 2026, Meridian held $6.4 billion cash with total debt of $2.1 billion. Free cash flow was $924 million, up 38% YoY.

Guidance: Q2 2026 revenue guided at $5.05B–$5.15B (21–23% YoY growth). Full-year 2026 guidance raised to $20.2B–$20.6B.

"We are extremely pleased with our Q1 performance," said CFO Daniel Hartman. "Our operational discipline is translating into meaningful margin expansion."

The Board approved a new $500 million share repurchase program.
---

Question: 这家公司本季度的营收同比增长是多少？只答百分比。

The following are two product reviews. Read them carefully and answer the question at the end.

---
Review of Product A: Apex Pro 15 Smartphone

The Apex Pro 15 sets a new bar for endurance. With a 6000mAh silicon-carbon battery and an adaptive 1Hz–144Hz display, this phone comfortably lasts two full days under typical mixed usage. In our standardized battery test (continuous HD video at 50% brightness), it lasted 28 hours and 41 minutes — the best result we've ever recorded from a flagship. The 80W fast charger refills from 0 to 100% in approximately 42 minutes. The camera, powered by a Sony LYT-900 sensor, delivers exceptional low-light performance. Build quality is premium with a titanium frame and Gorilla Glass Victus 3.

---
Review of Product B: Luminary Z9 Ultra

The Luminary Z9 Ultra is a performance powerhouse focused on gaming and photography, but battery life is its Achilles' heel. The 4800mAh cell is drained quickly by the always-on 165Hz display and power-hungry Snapdragon 8 Elite chip. In our standardized test, it managed just 14 hours and 22 minutes — well behind the competition. Real-world heavy users rarely make it through a full day. The 120W HyperCharge is impressive, filling the battery in under 30 minutes. Camera performance is outstanding with a 200MP main sensor. The display, at 3500 nits peak brightness, is the best we've tested.
---

Question: Which product has better battery life? Answer only A or B.

The following is an article about urban sustainability. Read it carefully and answer the question at the end.

---
BUILDING THE CITIES OF TOMORROW: URBAN SUSTAINABILITY IN 2026

As climate pressures intensify, cities worldwide are deploying a range of strategies to reduce emissions and build resilience. Here we examine four of the most impactful approaches.

Expanding Renewable Energy Infrastructure: Cities from Copenhagen to Shenzhen are aggressively transitioning to renewables. Solar panels now cover more than 40% of rooftops in select districts of Seoul and Singapore, while offshore wind capacity in European coastal cities has tripled since 2020. Many cities pair this with battery storage systems to maintain grid stability.

Green Rooftops and Urban Greening: Vegetation-covered rooftops are proliferating across urban cores, reducing the urban heat island effect and cutting building energy consumption by up to 25%. Paris, Toronto, and Melbourne have mandated green rooftop installations on new commercial buildings. Urban tree canopy programs have also expanded, with many cities targeting 30% canopy cover by 2030.

Electrification of Public Transit: The shift to electric buses and trams is dramatically reducing urban air pollution. Shenzhen operates the world's largest all-electric bus fleet with over 16,000 vehicles. London, Oslo, and Bogotá have set hard deadlines to fully electrify public bus fleets by 2030.

Smart Water Management: Forward-looking cities are deploying AI-driven sensor networks to detect pipe leaks in real time and recycle greywater for non-potable uses. Singapore's PUB has implemented a smart water grid covering 100% of its network, reducing non-revenue water loss below 5%.
---

Question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the article?
A) Renewable energy infrastructure
B) Green rooftops
C) Vertical indoor farming
D) Electric buses

Answer with only the letter.

The following are minutes from a product strategy meeting. Read them carefully and summarize the 3 main decisions using bullet points.

---
PRODUCT STRATEGY MEETING — MINUTES
Date: May 8, 2026 | 2:00 PM – 4:30 PM SGT
Attendees: Sarah Lin (CPO), James Okafor (VP Engineering), Rachel Tan (VP Marketing), David Wu (Head of Design), Priya Sharma (Finance Lead), Tom Eriksson (Head of Product)

AGENDA ITEM 1: Q3 ROADMAP REVIEW
The team reviewed five features in progress. Unified Search and the Onboarding Flow redesign are cleared for Q3 release. However, the AI Recommendations Engine remains below the internal accuracy threshold (71% vs. required 85%). The team voted unanimously to delay the AI Recommendations Engine from Q3 to Q4 2026, with a hard deadline of November 1.

AGENDA ITEM 2: MARKETING BUDGET REALLOCATION
Rachel Tan presented an updated spend proposal. Analysis showed performance marketing on social channels significantly outperforming paid search. The team approved increasing the total H2 2026 marketing budget by 15%, with all incremental spend directed to social and content channels. Priya Sharma confirmed Finance can accommodate this given Q1's stronger-than-expected revenue.

AGENDA ITEM 3: REMOTE WORK POLICY
The current hybrid policy (up to 4 remote days per week), set to expire June 30, 2026, was reviewed. An internal survey showed 78% of employees prefer continued flexibility. The team agreed to recommend extending the current policy through end of 2026. The recommendation will be submitted to the Executive Committee at next week's All-Hands.

AGENDA ITEM 4: DESIGN SYSTEM MIGRATION
David Wu proposed migrating from the legacy Figma-based design system to the new Prism Design System (WCAG 2.2 AA compliant, 40% faster component build times). The 12-week phased migration was approved in principle, with a detailed project plan due by May 22.
---

The following is an excerpt from a scientific study on sleep and cognitive performance. Read it carefully and answer the question at the end.

---
SLEEP DURATION AND NEXT-DAY COGNITIVE PERFORMANCE: A CONTROLLED STUDY
Authors: Dr. Yuki Tanaka, Dr. Chloe Bauer, Dr. Marcus Reid — Journal of Sleep Research, Vol. 14, No. 2, 2026

Abstract: This study investigated the relationship between sleep duration and next-day cognitive performance across memory, attention, and executive function. 120 participants aged 20–35 were randomly assigned to one of three conditions over 7 days: habitual sleep (7–9 hours), mild restriction (5–6 hours), and severe restriction (≤4 hours).

Methods: Participants were recruited from a university population and screened to exclude sleep disorders and shift workers. Sleep was monitored via polysomnography in a controlled lab on nights 1, 4, and 7; actigraphy on remaining nights. Memory was assessed via word-pair recall (immediate and delayed). Attention via Psychomotor Vigilance Task (PVT). Executive function via Trail Making Test Part B.

Results: The habitual sleep group maintained stable performance throughout. The mild restriction group showed significant PVT declines by Day 3 (p < 0.01) and delayed recall declines by Day 5 (p < 0.05). The severe restriction group demonstrated impairment across all three domains by Day 2, with PVT reaction times increasing by a mean of 38% compared to baseline. Subjective sleepiness ratings (Karolinska Sleepiness Scale) consistently underestimated objective impairment in both restriction groups. Recovery to baseline required a full three nights of unrestricted sleep, even after only one week of restriction.

Limitations: The laboratory setting may not reflect real-world conditions; participant age range limits generalizability to older adults.
---

Statement: "Participants who slept fewer than 6 hours showed a 40% reduction in memory retention."

Is this statement supported by the article, contradicted by the article, or not stated in the article? Answer with only: yes / no / not stated.

The following is an excerpt from a fictional family estate novel. Read it carefully and answer the question at the end.

---
The Hartwell estate had stood on the Somerset cliffs for three centuries, its stone façade worn smooth by Atlantic winds. On the morning of the will reading, the last of the family gathered in the great library beneath the portrait of old Edmund Hartwell, founder of the line.

At the head of the table sat Victor Hartwell, the eldest son of the late patriarch. Beside him sat his wife, Constance, who had never felt at home among the Hartwells. Across the table sat Eleanor Hartwell, Victor's younger sister, who had returned from twelve years in Hong Kong only two weeks before their father's death. The awkwardness between the siblings had not thawed.

The youngest person in the room was Thomas, the only child of Eleanor's first marriage. At twenty-four, he bore an unsettling resemblance to the old portrait above — the same angular jaw and watchful grey eyes. Thomas had grown up largely in the care of his maternal grandmother, seeing Eleanor only during summer visits.

Seated near the door was Harriet Bowden, the estate's solicitor. The will was simple: the estate and investments were to be divided equally between Victor and Eleanor. Thomas was to receive the contents of the library — eleven thousand volumes and his grandfather's private correspondence — a bequest that struck Constance as peculiar but Thomas as the most meaningful gift he had ever received.

What none of them yet knew was that buried within those eleven thousand volumes was a letter that would reopen questions the family had spent forty years burying.
---

Question: What is the relationship between Person X (Eleanor) and Person Y (Thomas)? Answer in one sentence.

以下是一段多轮对话。根据完整的对话历史，回答最后一条用户消息。最终问题只答数字。

用户：我现在有 1000 元。
助手：好的，我记住了——你现在有 1000 元。有什么需要帮助计算的吗？
用户：我花了 30% 买书，再花了剩下钱的四分之一买衣服。
助手：让我来算：买书花了 1000 × 30% = 300 元，剩余 700 元。买衣服花了 700 × 25% = 175 元，剩余 525 元。
用户：现在我还剩多少钱？请只答数字。
